dataTypes.name: ObjectMatch

properties.name: matchingObjectId
properties.documentation: Identifier for this Entity,

dataTypes.documentation: Reference

dataTypes.name: ObjectDetailRequest

properties.name: speed
properties.documentation: Match must be of a given speed or between bounds given by 2 speeds.

properties.name: course
properties.documentation: Match must be of a given course or between bounds given by 2 courses.

properties.name: dimension
properties.documentation: Any of the battle dimension specified. If none specified all battle dimensions are deemed to match.

properties.name: standard_identifer
properties.documentation: Any of the standard identities specified. If none specified all standard identities dimensions are deemed to match.

properties.name: position
properties.documentation: Object must be on the given position if given.

properties.name: source
properties.documentation: Any of the sources specified. If none specified all sources are deemed to match.

properties.name: creationTime
properties.documentation: Match must be of a given creation time or between bounds given by 2 creation times.

properties.name: quality
properties.documentation: Match must be of a given quality or between bounds given by 2 qualities.

properties.name: length
properties.documentation: Match must be of a given length or between bounds given by 2 lengths.

properties.name: polyArea
properties.documentation: Match must be within the bounds of the given area

dataTypes.documentation: Standard properties of a Tactical Object. All optional as these are the criteria used in a matching object request test matching objects against. Object must match all provided criteria to Match.

dataTypes.name: Requirement
dataTypes.documentation: Base representation of a Requirement to be requested from Component services.

dataTypes.name: Entity

properties.name: id
properties.documentation: Identifier for this Entity,

properties.name: source
properties.documentation: Identifier for the source of this Entity to allow traceability.

dataTypes.documentation:
Base Entity.
Allows all CRUD EntityActions.
This represents a piece of subject matter which has standardised semantics.
Any service using a specialisation of this type will always have standard (CRUD) exchange mechanics:
Create
Read
Update
Delete
The platform specific translation will implement these depending on the target protocol.

dataTypes.name: Constraint
dataTypes.documentation: Generic constraint representation, name <> value pair

properties.name: success
properties.documentation: success = true means I have done the requested action. ie. Created the requirement (not actioned) or deleted the requirement

properties.name: requirementId
properties.documentation: The id of the requirement this solution is for

dataTypes.name: Action
dataTypes.documentation: Work done as part of the solution such as doing an internal calculation or managing a dependency on another component

literals.name: locateSeaSurfaceObjects
literals.documentation: Use active Tracking to locate and provide evidence for the existence of Sea Surface Tactical Objects

literals.name: Obtain
literals.documentation: Actively collect new data to fulfill need

classes.name: Report Spot SAR Image Display Presentation
Report Strip SAR Image Display Presentation
ASMPT19 Report ISAR Image Display Presentation

properties.name: initRequest
properties.documentation: Is this component responsible for initiating the command (per command sent)

classes.name: TP Create Local Radar Track Context
classes.documentation: In Pyramid implementation this link is removed

classes.name: EntityActions
classes.documentation:
These semantic actions apply to each port. Usage example:

"Create Track" -> ObjectInterestRequirement entering TacticalObjects 
1. Radar_DisplayProcessing_HMIDialogue calls (via HMIObjectsBridge)-> create(ObjectRequirement)
2. TacticalObjects Responds with requirement identifier (means that requirement has been accepted but does not guarantee actioning. 
3. Radar_DisplayProcessing_HMIDialogue calls (via HMIObjectsBridge)->read(Query(identifier)) 
4. TacticalObjects streams updates on status of that requirement.
5. Radar_DisplayProcessing_HMIDialogue cancels read call when no longer required. 

In this way the status of the sent requirement shall be read by the sending components. This sequence shall be common to all components.

classes.name: HDI
classes.documentation: Short form Acronym for the HMI Dialogue component

classes.name: Request
classes.documentation: A statement of a goal intended to be achieved by a component

classes.name: Requirement
classes.documentation: An entity managed by a component with that defines the goal extracted from a request along, with the achievement progress towards the goal

classes.name: TOB
classes.documentation: Short form Acronym for the Tactical Objects component

classes.name: IPR
classes.documentation: Short form Acronym for the Information Presentation component

classes.name: Solution
classes.documentation: A plan produced by the component that will satisfy the goal defined in the associated requirement

classes.name: Actor
classes.documentation: An entity that place a request on a component

classes.name: SDI
classes.documentation: Short form Acronym for the Sensor Data Interpretation component

classes.name: SPR
